examples.title: Finding Generators of Cyclic Group Zn
examples.description: Algorithm to find all generators of Zn under addition modulo n
examples.explanation: A generator a produces all n elements when we compute a×k mod n for k=0 to n-1. For prime n, all non-zero elements generate. For n=8, only odd numbers 1,3,5,7 work because gcd(a,n)=1.

examples.title: Computing Order of Element in Zn
examples.description: Algorithm to find order of an element in cyclic group Zn
examples.code:
def order_of_element(a, n):
 """Find order of element a in Zn under addition modulo n"""
 current = a % n
 count = 1
 while current != 0:
 current = (current + a) % n
 count += 1
 if count > n: # Avoid infinite loop
 return float('inf')
 return count

# Examples
print("Order of 2 in Z8:", order_of_element(2, 8)) # 4
print("Order of 3 in Z7:", order_of_element(3, 7)) # 7
print("Order of 4 in Z6:", order_of_element(4, 6)) # 3
examples.explanation: Starting from a, repeatedly add a modulo n until we reach 0. The number of steps is the order. If order equals n, the element is a generator.

examples.title: Verify Group Properties for Custom Set
examples.description: Program to verify if a set with operation forms a group
examples.explanation: This code systematically checks all four group axioms: closure, associativity, identity, and inverses. It can verify whether any finite set with a defined operation forms a group.
